title: Cyathea haenkei
intro: Cyathea haenkei is a species of fern, specifically a tree fern, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as support the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Cyathea haenkei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cyathea haenkei is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Cyathea haenkei is classified as a species of tree fern in the genus Cyathea, within the family Cyatheaceae. This classification places it in the group known as Polypodiopsida, or the false ferns. Further, it is also classified as a member of the subclass Cyathea, which is part of the class Polypodiopsida. Additionally, it belongs to the subclass Cyatheae, which is part of the order Cyatheales. It is a part of the larger division Pteridophyta, or vascular ferns.

The plant's fronds have been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions and wounds. In addition, its roots are said to be used to treat fever and other illnesses. The plant's medicinal uses are primarily based on its bioactive compounds, which are being studied for their potential health benefits.
